title: linum tenuifolium var. differens
intro: Linum tenuifolium var. differens, also known as the narrow-leaved flax or golden flax, is a variety of the flax plant that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Linum tenuifolium var. differens include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, and are often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
